title: A bill to require the Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service to reissue a final rule removing the gray wolf from the list of endangered and threatened wildlife under the Endangered Species Act of 1973.
plain_summary: This bill tells the head of the Fish and Wildlife Service to bring back a rule that takes gray wolves off the endangered species list. The person in charge has sixty days to do this. Once the rule comes back, no one can take it to court to fight against it. This means gray wolves would no longer be protected as endangered animals.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.

sections.title: Section 1  Reissuance of Rule Removing the Gray Wolf From the List of Endangered and Threatened Wildlife
sections.text: The Director of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service must bring back a rule that removes gray wolves from the list of animals that are protected because they are in danger of becoming extinct. This rule was originally published on November third, two thousand and twenty in volume eighty-five of the Federal Register on page sixty-nine thousand seven hundred seventy-eight. The Director has sixty days after this law is passed to bring this rule back into effect. Once the Director brings back this rule, no one can challenge it in court.
